# Note Events as a Table

## Lesson Goal

Represent transcription output as an inspectable table.

## What to Notice

Tables are the debugging bridge between raw audio and notation.

## Work With the Notebook

- [Open the notebook source](https://github.com/buildLittleWorlds/marimo-audio-self-tutorial/blob/main/notebooks/11_note_event_table/notebook.py)
- [Run the browser notebook](https://buildlittleworlds.github.io/marimo-audio-self-tutorial/notebooks/11_note_event_table/)

Run locally:

```bash
uv run marimo edit notebooks/11_note_event_table/notebook.py
```

## Feedback Lens

Look for whether the student can read a note-event table before jumping to sheet music.

## Instructor Notes

Use this lesson as a small checkpoint. Before asking students to add features,
ask them to name the input, the transformation, the output, and the current
limitation. That habit will matter more in later transcription notebooks than
any one line of code.
